event.title: performed -in 1819

data.event: ;In accocrnt of a Tour mode to lay down $he Course and Le- vels of the River Setlg or Satu'dr6, a$ .far as traceabb within the limits of t/je Brjt~h auQority, performed -in 1819, . . . d a.8 BY CAPT. J. D. HERBERT; ~ T H RPGT.

event.title: 1819 in the caursa of the aurvey operations in which I wpa

data.event: N. I. I N 1819 in the caursa of the aurvey operations in which I wpa theq m-' gaged I traced the River Setlej to the confines of the British authorih.

event.title: September, I quitted

data.event: On t&e lat September, I quitted K-b.

event.title: crossed the latter, which is amongst the largest of the mom- tain rivers that have not their origin immediately from the snowy chain, We were now

data.event: t , A fter,crossmg the pass, we proceeded down the Salar stream, a# feeder of &e Giri, and crossed the latter, which is amongst the largest of the mom- tain rivers that have not their origin immediately from the snowy chain, We were now on the right bank of this river, and in the hill state of Kyun- thal.

event.title: had reached the river head, and at3 we had now

data.event: The evening ww far advanced before we had reached the river head, and at3 we had now attained an elevation at which Are-wood ceases to be procurable, it became necessary to halt at this place, which hact also the, advantage of affording b degree of shelter to our followers in some ayes w d overhanging rocks.

event.title: now met

data.event: Snow b d e of eome extent were now met with, shewing we mere approaching the pass, and the fiequent occnrf6nce of the black and red' currant, with the birch, indicated an elevation very near the limit of forest, and consequently led ue to believe that the river head (judging from ana- legy) could not be far.

event.title: exceed 13,000 feet, so great was the cold even at this season

data.event: Although the elevation did not much exceed 13,000 feet, so great was the cold even at this season of the year,.

event.title: Undisturbed by anxiety, we now

data.event: , . . 1 Undisturbed by anxiety, we now found ourselves with sufficient le'rsnre ta observe and to enjoy this singular scene.

event.title: now close to objects so often viewed

data.event: Their nearness and consequent great apparent magnitude, the idea that we were now close to objects so often viewed from great dis- tances, and which had so often exercised conjecture ; these and a thousand other circumstances gave an interest to the scene, that it is difficult to corn- ' municate by any description.

event.title: took up our quarters

data.event: we arossed the Baspa, the river above noticed, on a well,boarded, and railed sanga , . ninety-one feet in length, and took up our quarters in the nearer of the two villages, Sangla.

event.title: ~zrgunnah of Hangareng, also subject to Bissahir, with Ladak, l and with KGllG, a mountain state situate on the right bank of the Setlej, "and now

data.event: cast it is contern~inous with the Chinese.possessions, and on the west wit11 'the Tartar p~zrgunnah of Hangareng, also subject to Bissahir, with Ladak, l and with KGllG, a mountain state situate on the right bank of the Setlej, "and now subject to Runjeet Sinh.

event.title: visited in August, 1818

data.event: There is a pass beyond Cliil- kul, to Nilang, on the JBranbbi, (a place I, visited in August, 1818,) by a route .leading u/) the river bed.

event.title: four days (from ' . - ... 8 s

data.event: Be travelled four days (from ' . - ... 8 s ...

event.title: three day's snow, and lastly two of descent .to MGkba on the Bhagirathi; fmm Mhkba to Nilang, his route coincided

data.event: 356 COURSE AND LEVELS Chilkul) before he reached the head of the river, then* ascending the pass he had three day's snow, and lastly two of descent .to MGkba on the Bhagirathi; fmm Mhkba to Nilang, his route coincided with mine.

event.title: presented to be . but , six days

data.event: Cbtiprang is re- presented to be . but , six days journey ( f p 1oaded.sheep.) from Sbipki ; from Nilang they represent: it but.

event.title: are stntianed there two officers of Usang and two linndred soldiers ; at oilier seasons

data.event: Gdnl is only frecjl~ented, lie says, by the slieplicrtlb dr~ring ttie season of the rains, when tlre pasturage is good, at which time are stntianed there two officers of Usang and two linndred soldiers ; at oilier seasons t6ey remove to sncrh- places as aflord tlie best pasturage.

event.title: stated to be fifteen days

data.event: 'G!lru, Ile stated to be fifteen days journey lience.

event.title: now ,codme within I sight . , . . of Marang, a ,div,ision d

data.event: We had now ,codme within I sight . , . . of Marang, a ,div,ision d six hamlets . . spread out on the

event.title: three months absent, and seven of their sheep had died

data.event: They had been three months absent, and seven of their sheep had died on the road,, a duty of two pice per load was collected from them at Ritang, where a Chinese Sirdar resides.

event.title: fofthed by the numerous torrents that spring

data.event: To have a correct idea of these places it must be borne in mind, that at this time we were proceeding along the'de- elivity of the great snowy range : so lofty a range, it may be'mpposed, can- not rise hm, so low a level as the river has here, with the undeviating recl gularity comprehended in the terms slope or declivity; on the contrary it is necessary to view the Himalaya mountains themselves, those eldest horn of creation, to estimate even approximately the gigantic scale on which the furrows or cavines fofthed by the numerous torrents that spring from their snows, intersect their sides.

event.title: the last year sheltered

data.event: The cave in which the Mu- rang people had the last year sheltered themselves, had disappeared, and in- stead of it we beheld the fragments of fallen peaks, the ruinous proofs of the vast power of the avalanche.

event.title: set out a little befofe seven, but, in consequence of the fatiguing march- es of the two preceding days

data.event: We set out a little befofe seven, but, in consequence of the fatiguing march- es of the two preceding days, did not deem it advisable to proceed more' than six miles and a half, to Hopeha Wodar, a halting place (no'village,) on the' banks of a stream.

event.title: occupies nine days

data.event: The journey occupies nine days, or if a horseman travels, five.

event.title: days journey frqm Slhipki, a p 1 ~ 0 pr~ %stapes a-be&, and tk

data.event: w b u ia three month journey: M-ovar is eightepp days journey frqm Slhipki, a p 1 ~ 0 pr~ %stapes a-be&, and tk bouqdqy of ow mowrain p q p ~ i o m ~ h o w

event.title: trade with Shipki, Meyang and Chiprang, for salt and wool ; that Chbprnng is nine days

data.event: He had been a great traveller, and we found him very communicative ; we deter- mined, as he was the only person we could find capable of acttng as an in- terpreter, to make him accompany us lo Shipki ; he told us that they trade with Shipki, Meyang and Chiprang, for salt and wool ; that Chbprnng is nine days journey from Shipki and Mansarowar twenty ; on horseback, however, the former journey is performed in four or five days, and the latter io twelve.

event.title: is performed in four or five days

data.event: He had been a great traveller, and we found him very communicative ; we deter- mined, as he was the only person we could find capable of acttng as an in- terpreter, to make him accompany us lo Shipki ; he told us that they trade with Shipki, Meyang and Chiprang, for salt and wool ; that Chbprnng is nine days journey from Shipki and Mansarowar twenty ; on horseback, however, the former journey is performed in four or five days, and the latter io twelve.

event.title: four months journey from Shipki, In the evening we were much amused

data.event: TeshG LombG is three months, and Latsa four months journey from Shipki, In the evening we were much amused with a dance to which these pew pie invited us.

event.title: ktrred would be th$ limit to our travels in this quarter

data.event: ' From Namja, our next stage was Shipki, which we ktrred would be th$ limit to our travels in this quarter, although at Dabliq and at N a m j ~ we were strongly assured that orders had been received to conduct us to Ghu; The road to Shipki was tolerably good, with the exception of one vbry steep and deep descent through a narrow defile, huge rocks, like buttresses or tow- ers, overhanging the path.

event.title: now upon the' t3lrdold of -the celestial empire, and though in part prepared

data.event: We were now upon the' t3lrdold of -the celestial empire, and though in part prepared for Tar- thr'featttrts atld otherbpecotiarities, we stilI found much that was new and -striking.

event.title: offered us a Nez- zer of one day

data.event: enquiring into the truth of the report of orders hayingarrived to: cons duct us to GQru, it proved (as, I had conjectured) to ,have no foundati- on ; the people were however very c;lvil, and . . the Seyana offered us a Nez- zer of one day's . provisions . for all OUT followers.

event.title: halt five'daya for his answer ; that during those five days

data.event: 'we should write to the Deba at GB~II, and halt five'daya for his answer ; that during those five days they would furnish us with provisions gratis, if 'we chose to decline paying for them, and that we should be bound by the answer either to proceed or feturn.

event.title: two days journey distaht for ti horseman ; the distance occupies

data.event: There are two stations, the Wintet and the Summer, which are two days journey distaht for ti horseman ; the distance occupies seven or eight days .on foot.

event.title: is relieved every seven years

data.event: G h ~ t , er ratter between the two governments, for the D e b is relieved every seven years.

event.title: sixteen days journey

data.event: ,It is fifteen or sixteen days journey &am Giiru, .although on horseback it may be gerformd in five or six day+ From Skalkar in ?Hangarasg 1tber.e is a nearer road svhich leads :tl~rgql~ tthe .purgunnah of Spiti in Iadalc, Leb is situated bout north fmnp S h i p 6 Ghnu about east.

event.title: w , a of Mary tm, a v jllage, f9.w days

data.event: He w , a of Mary tm, a v jllage, f9.w days journey beyond Bfamarowar, .his namechang Ring, Jing. .Mansarowar, be told us, is.a snowy range ; the lake being called Matalae, hit t4is waet have be@ a ruistake, as .Sarowar signifies the same,aa Tdae. .uo;wever i menti00 it as it rnay,ser~e,to t 8 r o ~ .light OP .the story of the Dablix Lam@ as to four rivers origi4atin.g ,there.

event.title: ahwt ej&ht,or,& days

data.event: The Spiti river, or right branch of .the Setlej, . . he saps,, r,bes ahwt ej&ht,or,& days journey .fioq .ip .qodjuence with tbe .lea ,braqQ,

event.title: is aoaaumed, At two year

data.event: 4m T h e Tartar ahtpherds do not live in villages but ia tents; t h y Iead a wandering life, removing from place to place, &-the pasturage is aoaaumed, At two year's old the -wool is in greatest qnantiq and perfection, and th4 r a m furnish the k t . &k and Mamo are the naates of the ram and ~ W B , urd the wool k called pul or pal, but in this quarter it ia termed C h a n g w und the mountaineers call it Byangi hn.

event.title: called pul or pal, but in this quarter

data.event: 4m T h e Tartar ahtpherds do not live in villages but ia tents; t h y Iead a wandering life, removing from place to place, &-the pasturage is aoaaumed, At two year's old the -wool is in greatest qnantiq and perfection, and th4 r a m furnish the k t . &k and Mamo are the naates of the ram and ~ W B , urd the wool k called pul or pal, but in this quarter it ia termed C h a n g w und the mountaineers call it Byangi hn.

event.title: resides a Giiryan, or governor, - From Ghrn to Manearowar is eight day

data.event: At G k u resides a Giiryan, or governor, - From Ghrn to Manearowar is eight day's journey.

event.title: thle lowest of these mountains had an elwatioa of 13'; this mountaid bore 1180

data.event: to 900 extended a chain ofmountains of smooth rounded form, bare of forest or snow, and clothed with a withered or russet vegetation, bug nothing like a Table Land or &in, or even valley of any- Went; thle lowest of these mountains had an elwatioa of 13'; this mountaid bore 1180 or 26" S. pf E. To the nortb, appeared a cluster bf irregulap peaks sharp, bare b d rocky, bat scarcely rising above .the limit of snow ; the wa- t t ~ ~ of tile Setlej yere visible for sejereral miles ; the depression was at tllc hrthennoet visible point 80 and the bearing 85' or 5" N. of E ;'it seemed as far as I was able to judge, from the run of the mountains, to bend round korp the southward.

event.title: proceed, and as the season! was now

data.event: us little, hope of being alloweti ta proceed, and as the season! was now far advanced, w2e began to.

event.title: had been delivered to the Kardar of the Zumpung of Chiprang who was at Sk;rng;ze, four days

data.event: The letter had been delivered to the Kardar of the Zumpung of Chiprang who was at Sk;rng;ze, four days at least wonld elapse Befire the Garpang could send 9 answer, and.

event.title: is sown in April

data.event: Wheat is sown in April, A small trade is carried on between thii place and tbe people of f(,s- +wer; salt and byangi woolis exchanged for several W a

event.title: said, whither we were now

data.event: Beyond Skalkar, he said, whither we were now proceeding, was still another village belonging to Bissahir, called 8Gmra.

event.title: noticed a curious appearance in this day

data.event: Nako contains about thirty houses, and is situated on the western declivity of the PGrkyGl mountain; there are a few apricot trees, poplars and oziers, and barley and wheat, are grown still higher than the village, tlio~igh its elevation be nearly 10,000 feet, ' We noticed a curious appearance in this day's march, which we had af- terwards occasion to observe, was very common in this part of the country.

event.title: now flows

data.event: Chang is situated in the middle of a little flat, which might be almost called a valley ; .it is in some measure, or has been, the bed of a torrent, but the stream now flows deep below and far away to the right.

event.title: had come from Leh to Lari, a village two or three marches ahead, in fifteen days

data.event: He had come from Leh to Lari, a village two or three marches ahead, in fifteen days, and he informed- us that Runjeet Sinh had established a Wakil at Leh, and that the country.

event.title: k also a road h e n c ~ to RGpeho, five day

data.event: There k also a road h e n c ~ to RGpeho, five day's journey, of about ei&t or n i ~ e milee each, Prom Rlpsho, @fitah is twenty day's joarney, and Leh tPn ; tiam Skalkar represented to :be a few miles ahead, Lad is two day% journey, and Dadcar f ~ f i , eve.

event.title: twenty day's joarney, and Leh tPn ; tiam Skalkar represented

data.event: There k also a road h e n c ~ to RGpeho, five day's journey, of about ei&t or n i ~ e milee each, Prom Rlpsho, @fitah is twenty day's joarney, and Leh tPn ; tiam Skalkar represented to :be a few miles ahead, Lad is two day% journey, and Dadcar f ~ f i , eve.

event.title: now reached

data.event: We had now reached the thirtp-second degree of latitude, and had I& the tm Himalap far to the &ward, while a new country of entirely a dif- fmnt aspect lay b e h e us.

event.title: winter, induced

data.event: The apprehension that we should find some of the passes shut, apd the uncertainty under which we laboured as to our being able finally tp reach any poiat where me could winter, induced us at last, however unwil- , lingly, to resolve on returning.

event.title: was determined that mT companion ahould halt the next day

data.event: The difiiculty, too, which we felt on account of provisiops, was an additional inducement; and it was determined that mT companion ahould halt the next day, and on the following, commence his re- &urn, while I, with a very few followem, should push on to the first Lataki village, from which I hoped by forced marches to overtake him.

event.title: a day urged

data.event: ,My wish however to gain a day urged me to make the attempt, and, with the support of two muscular Tartars, I forded the Spiti river.

event.title: falls ; in May
ascends, for five and three quarter

data.event: The path gradually ascends, for five and three quarter miles, to Che- jang Kanka, a pass over one of the lateraI ridges which shut in the numer- ous streams that feed the river.

event.title: a day; the summit of this pass is a level piece of some extent, and we found

data.event: These ridges are all ofgreat height, or rather the beds of the streams are of great depth, and to cross even one of them is nearly the labour of a day; the summit of this pass is a level piece of some extent, and we found eome huts and an attempt at cultivation.

event.title: now perceived

data.event: T h e steepness of the opposite bank, and great height, seemed to defy all access to it, but the rear of my com-, ' panion's line of march, which was now perceived slowly ascending it, proved that it was to be surmounted, and gave us hopes of overtaking them before evening.

event.title: contsina nine villages, the revenue of which is but 990 Rupees a year

data.event: It contsina nine villages, the revenue of which is but 990 Rupees a year ; a spall trade is carried on with Lad& m d Gertop, to both which places there are good maas.

event.title: is sown in March

data.event: Wheat is sown in March,' and cut in July.

event.title: cut in July

data.event: Wheat is sown in March,' and cut in July.

event.title: occurs: from Manes to Dankar, is three day

data.event: On this side of the range, Rupa is the lad village met with, and on the other side Manes, a village of Ladak, is the first which occurs: from Manes to Dankar, is three day's

event.title: exchanged their hue of green for the more dazzling, though not so pleasing, livery of winter

data.event: On looking about us we found it was but too true, and that the snow lay about half a cubit deep ail round our tents, while tlie sur- rounding ridges and peaks had all exchanged their hue of green for the more dazzling, though not so pleasing, livery of winter.

event.title: now the minter had evidently set

data.event: The great object, now the minter had evidently set in, was to get beyond the high ridge separating these two village% after which we should have no very high groupd to traverse, and ahould consequently be more independent of changes in the weather, The path leads down the Rushkalang for four miles, an easy descent and generally good, it then crosses tho'river on a sanga, thirty-three feet long and twenty-five above the stream.

event.title: now got

data.event: We had now got back to the region of forest, and the noble pines through which our route lay, at once gave the scenery its principal charm, and afforded us the real advantage of shade.

event.title: now learned

data.event: We now learned there was a road up its bed leading to Bekar, and a small hamlet on one of its banks, a daj's journey from the Setlej.

event.title: had seen so much' of this kind in the upper part of Kanhwer, that these features had now

data.event: Naked and lofty precipices, or bare and broken declivities, present little to attract , attention, after the -first edge of wonder and fear is worn off; and we had seen so much' of this kind in the upper part of Kanhwer, that these features had now lost their power over us.

event.title: now start

data.event: full with horror," and " direness familiar to our thoughts could not now start us.", Thermometer 420.

event.title: ran is the summer

data.event: Se- ran is the summer residence of the Raja of Bissahir who m o v e 8 here to.

event.title: a good deal as yesterday

data.event: The road upon the nhaie is tolerable and the general appearance of the river valley a good deal as yesterday.

event.title: this season of the year is not seen

data.event: The sun at this season of the year is not seen till past ten o'clock, so that the mornings feel.

event.title: mornings feel

data.event: The sun at this season of the year is not seen till past ten o'clock, so that the mornings feel.

event.title: days we were much incommoded

data.event: The two following days we were much incommoded by it, The 12' might be called the last day of the fair which had only lasted two days, though three is the term generally allowed.

event.title: the last day of the fair which had only lasted

data.event: The two following days we were much incommoded by it, The 12' might be called the last day of the fair which had only lasted two days, though three is the term generally allowed.

event.title: encounter the heats which even at this season

data.event: Our cmstitutions having been so long accustomed to the bnc- dng air of the Himalaya tract, were little prepared te encounter the heats which even at this season prevail in a river valley, so narrow and so deep ae this of the Setlej.

event.title: found'agreeable, and a few days

data.event: The temperature of Kotgerh however which is be- tween 3 and 4000 feet bigher, we found'agreeable, and a few days affer our arrival we had a heavy fall of snow.

event.title: will be pro- per therefore now

data.event: It will be pro- per therefore now to give aa idea of the manner in which these two ob- jects were accomplished and also to indicate some of the results.

event.title: September 6 . w

data.event: - ~ u g w t 10 14 26 81 September 6 . w Octobrr 1 .

event.title: now compare

data.event: We may now compare the above result of longitude with that giveh ' by the Clironometer.

event.title: 27 days, is seen

data.event: The rate from Kotgerh to DBdt 27 days, is seen to be 79 24'losing.

event.title: kept very carefully at Saharanpur that the Maximum annual range is only 6 inches and in any one month

data.event: I find from a register kept very carefully at Saharanpur that the Maximum annual range is only 6 inches and in any one month not more than 4 inches.

event.title: now oply remains

data.event: It now oply remains that I should notice briefly the principal resulfs.

event.title: gives a fall of 51

data.event: The distance being 53 (or correcttd 6-21 gives a fall of 51 a feet n'erirly.

event.title: infer that it has a further fall of 1000

data.event: The present survey of . the . river terminated at Sli~ll but j~ldging from the qalogx of other rivers, I infer that it has a further fall of 1000 feet to ~200, before it . reaches the plains, in a course of about 36 miles.

event.title: Months seem

data.event: The Months seem to be similarly formed thus :- Hindi.

event.title: now before me aa alphabet (or ka, kha, as he called

data.event: I have now before me aa alphabet (or ka, kha, as he called it) written by the Lama Ring Jing d Dahling, and comparing it with that given in Yates's Sanskrit grammar ihe rmemblance is very k k i n g ; There are hoaever differences, chiefly unim- I portant ones in the aanner of forming #he .characters.

event.title: ~ a r o - wer is severr days

data.event: Rebgealing and h h g are both on the Setlej; Gh4pmag is opposite the former ; M Q ~ a r o - wer is severr days from Chiiprang ; 'Fokbo is the name of the G6r6 Pergen- nah.

event.title: one day's journey from the lake gives

data.event: Miinsarower and Man Tul4i.-Chang Ring Jing, a Beopari of Maryh, a village one day's journey from the lake gives the follgaing prticitlars.

event.title: bas its source in the high range KCngtim La; five day

data.event: The Lidang bas its source in the high range KCngtim La; five day's journey from Dankar.
